assets/hero/chummer6-hero.png.visual_prompt: First-contact Shadowrun cover scene in a bright streetdoc shack or converted clinic bay where a runner is getting a new cyberarm fitted, not an alley and not a flat prop layout. The runner sits in a hacked repair recliner while a visibly augmented streetdoc or cybertech calibrates the implant with surgical clamps, implant trays, tool wall, med rig, chrome arm parts, stained gauze, cheap med trash, bottles, and proof anchors around them. Runner-facing AR text is allowed only when it is useful and anchored to the implant work: NERVE SYNC, JOINT SEAL, GRIP TEST, PAIN WATCH, TORQUE LIMIT. The frame must feel dense, layered, desperate, and immediately specific to cyberarm trust, streetdoc risk, and Chummer-style visible reasoning. Push it toward packed flashy cover energy: stronger orange-cyan contrast, harder rim light, bright clinic lamps, vivid color spill, grime, ugly clinic fatigue, and obvious foreground-midground-background separation. If the outside world is visible, let it hint at a recognizable lore place such as Chicago after Bug City or an Arcology-shadow district. Show metahuman bodies, visible chrome, and social damage without splatter spectacle. Strong foreground props, readable midground posture, rich background clues, no shop signs, no flat prop layout, no desk glamour, no cards or paper in hand, no phone hero prop, no empty darkness, and no quiet side-profile portrait.

scene_contract.subject: a runner and a visibly augmented streetdoc deciding whether the new cyberarm fit is safe enough
scene_contract.environment: a bright streetdoc shack or converted clinic bay packed with surgical clamps, implant trays, a tool wall, chrome cyberarm parts, med rig, stained gauze, bottles, cheap med waste, and hard practical spill
scene_contract.action: calibrating nerve sync, joint seal, grip response, pain state, and torque limits while proof anchors live through the room around them
scene_contract.mood: tense, ugly, and high-pressure instead of brooding

A visibly augmented courier, GM, or quartermaster leans over a scarred hardcase and data chip under vivid sodium orange, acid cyan, magenta, and clinic-white practical light, with wet reflective tape, sealed pouches, med trash, chipped tool rails, and a route arrow on the floor. AR must make sense to a runner seeing it through smart glasses: translucent hazard brackets locked to case edges, a provenance halo around the chip, a route arrow anchored to the wet floor, and wrist-sync rails tied to visible cyberware. No readable words, no letters, no logos, no title card, no giant warning text.

assets/horizons/runsite.png.visual_prompt: Pre-run staging scene in a damp Sixth World service room, back hall, or warehouse edge where the mission space is becoming legible before the shooting starts. One ork, troll, or obviously augmented human planner with a cyberarm, datajack, trode rig, or smartlink glove should be tracing ingress paths and chokepoints across a diegetic AR wall map or projected floor-plan anchored to wet concrete, crate edges, and doorway seams instead of to a clean holo slab. Seed the room with real Shadowrun residue: a cropped Bug City containment-tower photo or skyline glimpse, devil rat bait tin, clipped critter snapshot, a scuffed megacorp hardware shell from Ares, Renraku, Evo, or Shiawase, cheap stim patches, stained gauze, and one exhausted teammate in the depth of frame. Make it feel adult, tired, and one mistake away from violence, with grime, sickness, rat pressure, and hard fluorescent or sodium light. Grounded cinematic realism, no readable text, no VTT tokens, no top-down boardgame map, no clean conference room, and no giant empty wall.

scene_contract.subject: a worn runner or planner turning a dangerous space into a readable route before the team commits
scene_contract.environment: a damp service room, warehouse edge, or safehouse wall with wet concrete, crate stacks, door seams, practical light, small Sixth World debris, and a diegetic AR route projection
scene_contract.action: marking ingress lanes, chokepoints, and fallback routes directly onto the physical space while the rest of the run still feels close and ugly
scene_contract.mood: hard-lived, tactical, dirty, and impatient with bad plans
scene_contract.easter_egg_placement: on a crate edge or route projector housing near the lower-right third
scene_contract.easter_egg_detail: classic Chummer troll sticker on a beat-up planning prop
scene_contract.easter_egg_visibility: visible but not louder than the route wall

assets/horizons/shadow-market.png.visual_prompt: Compatibility market scene with vendor stalls and package cards, some trustworthy and some clearly cursed, discovery and moderation shown through real objects and signage, with one sarcastic little no-troll-haggling sign on a side stall.

scene_contract.easter_egg_placement: on a vendor CRT display in the upper-right third
scene_contract.easter_egg_detail: classic Chummer troll used as a tiny looping market mascot

assets/horizons/tactical-pulse.png.visual_prompt: Active-session tactical awareness scene: one shared view keeps threats, penalties, and team status coherent while players stay in flow, table tension first, UI second.

scene_contract.easter_egg_placement: on the edge of the shared-view tablet in the lower-right third
scene_contract.easter_egg_detail: classic Chummer troll as a worn decal on the shared tactical tablet

assets/horizons/threadcutter.png.visual_prompt: Mod-conflict intervention scene: two clever package changes collide on one preview surface while rollback and compare options stay close at hand, conflict drama without generic code art.

scene_contract.easter_egg_placement: on the lower bezel of the diff-view laptop near the center
scene_contract.easter_egg_detail: classic Chummer troll sticker on the conflict preview machine
scene_contract.easter_egg_visibility: easy to notice on the banner
